title: Notes from the Field: Hepatitis B Virus Transmission Associated with Assisted Blood Glucose Monitoring in a Skilled Nursing Facility â North Carolina, 2024 | MMWR

Guidance from the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) recommends universal hepatitis B vaccination for persons aged <60 years ( 3 ). Based on shared clinical decision making, ACIP also recommends hepatitis B vaccination for adults aged ≥60 years at higher risk for hepatitis B, such as those with diabetes ( 3 ). Clinicians considering providing HBV vaccination to adults aged ≥60 years with diabetes should consider the risk posed by AMBG in skilled nursing facilities ( 4 ). Hepatitis B vaccination coverage remains low among persons aged ≥60 years with diabetes ( 5 ). The findings of this investigation highlight AMBG as a risk factor for HBV transmission and provide evidence that skilled nursing facilities might be an appropriate setting to offer hepatitis B vaccination.
